{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/38559302?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kamil-sawicki/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kamil-sawicki/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kamil-sawicki/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kamil-sawicki/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/kamil-sawicki", "id": 38559302, "login": "kamil-sawicki", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjM4NTU5MzAy", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kamil-sawicki/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kamil-sawicki/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kamil-sawicki/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kamil-sawicki/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/kamil-sawicki/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/kamil-sawicki", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-49283", "cvss": {"score": 8.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 8.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-295", "name": "Improper Certificate Validation"}], "description": "## Summary

SimpleSAMLphp's HTTP-Artifact receive path can treat an unsigned embedded SAML `Response` as cryptographically valid for the wrong IdP.

In the `HTTPArtifact::receive()` flow, the SOAP `ArtifactResponse` receives a TLS-based validator from `SOAPClient::addSSLValidator()`. The embedded SAML `Response` then receives a validator that delegates signature validation to that outer `ArtifactResponse`. Later, the SP validates the embedded `Response` against metadata selected from the embedded response issuer, not necessarily the artifact issuer.

The critical issue is that `SOAPClient::validateSSL()` returns normally when the TLS public key does not match the key currently being validated. `SAML2\Message::validate()` treats any validator call that does not throw an exception as successful. As a result, an `ArtifactResponse` obtained from one IdP can validate an unsigned embedded SAML `Response` that claims to be issued by a different IdP.

In a multi-IdP/federation deployment where a malicious or lower-trust IdP can issue an HTTP-Artifact response to an SP, this can allow the attacker to authenticate to the SP as arbitrary users from a higher-trust victim IdP.

## Impact

A malicious or lower-trust IdP in the same SP/federation trust set can authenticate to the SP as users from another IdP when HTTP-Artifact is used. The attacker can choose assertion attributes, `NameID`, and session data in the forged unsigned assertion.

This is an authentication bypass and identity-provider impersonation issue. In realistic federations, the security boundary between IdPs matters: a compromised or low-assurance IdP should not be able to mint identities for a high-assurance IdP.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-6929-8p9f-26jx", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-07-02T20:25:56Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6929-8p9f-26jx", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-6929-8p9f-26jx"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-49283"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-07-02T20:25:56Z", "references": ["https://github.com/simplesamlphp/saml2/security/advisories/GHSA-6929-8p9f-26jx", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6929-8p9f-26jx"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/simplesamlphp/saml2/security-advisories/GHSA-6929-8p9f-26jx", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/simplesamlphp/saml2", "summary": "SimpleSAMLphp HTTP-Artifact TLS validator confusion allows cross-IdP authentication bypass", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-07-02T20:25:57Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-6929-8p9f-26jx", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "6.2.1", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "simplesamlphp/saml2"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 6.0.0, < 6.2.1"}, {"first_patched_version": "5.0.6", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "simplesamlphp/saml2"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 5.0.0, < 5.0.6"}, {"first_patched_version": "4.20.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "simplesamlphp/saml2"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 4.20.2"}, {"first_patched_version": "4.20.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "simplesamlphp/saml2-legacy"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 4.20.2"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: SimpleSAMLphp HTTP-Artifact TLS validator confusion allows cross-IdP authentication bypass
